sound_type: silent / ripple in shallow glacial stream
ecosystem: glacial stream (cold, clear, high-gradient, mountain and coastal streams fed by glacial meltwater)

taxonomy.note: FIRST Salvelinus (char genus) in engine. Distinguishing feature: light spots on dark background (reverse of most trout). Separated from Bull Trout (degree 240) by genetic research in 1980s-90s.

two_subspecies.name: S. malma malma
two_subspecies.range: Northern: Kuskokwim River north/west through Bering region and into Asia

two_subspecies.name: S. malma lordi
two_subspecies.range: Southern: Washington, Oregon, British Columbia -- the PNW form documented in this entry

coloration: Olive-dark green back, pale yellow/cream/orange-red spots on sides. Spawning males: vivid orange-red spots intensifying against dark background.

name_origin.character: Dolly Varden, blacksmith's daughter, known for colorful spotted dress
name_origin.note: Victorian 'Dolly Varden' fashion style (polka-dotted fabric) also gave name to hats, cakes, and this spotted char.

components: gravel shimmer as fish holds in current
sound_profile.energy: E=2 -- acoustic minimum of a living fish in cold, clear water

habitat.note: Prefers cold tributary streams; sensitive to sediment and temperature increase from land use

ecology.spawning: Fall redds in cold tributary gravel; 500-2000 eggs per female

conservation.wa_status: Species of Concern in portions of range
conservation.federal_listing: Not listed (unlike Bull Trout which is Threatened)
conservation.protection: Olympic National Park, North Cascades National Park protect core habitat

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST Salvelinus (char genus) in engine. Chars: light spots on dark background, opposite of most trout. Dolly Varden and Bull Trout (240) were classified as one species until 1980s-90s genetic separation. Cold-water specialists across North Pacific Rim.

E=2 near-silence: the acoustic minimum of a cold-water predator in a glacial stream. The 'sound' is water displacement and fin breaks. Maximum contrast with S36 241 (The Sonics, E=8) -- spread of 6, among the widest in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: E=2 acoustic profile and E=8 contrast pairing.

theory_nodes.concept: Penultimate Salmonidae entry (241 of 7 in arc 236-242). At degree 243, engine crosses to Petromyzontidae -- jawless fish, 450M years. Dolly Varden closes the Salmonidae run one degree before the ancient fish transition.
theory_nodes.description: Dolly Varden as penultimate Salmonidae and transition marker.

cross_references.relationship: Sister species. Both Salvelinus. Classified as same species until 1980s-90s. Bull Trout more interior; Dolly Varden more coastal and glacial.
Shared Salmonidae arc. Dolly Varden follows salmon egg dislodging in streams -- a minor but natural ecological relationship.
Osprey predates Dolly Varden. Cold-stream salmonids are key prey across the Salmonidae arc.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=8 vs E=2 -- spread of 6. Maximum proto-punk aggression against near-silent glacial char. The degree holds both the scream and the stream simultaneously.
